title: Selective Monotone Booster For Dry-Regime Hazard
summary: XGBoost documents that monotonic constraints can improve predictive performance when strong directional priors are valid.
rationale: XGBoost documents that monotonic constraints can improve predictive performance when strong directional priors are valid. In dry green-flag-like regimes, pit hazard should usually rise with tyre-age pressure, finish infeasibility, and degradation pressure; the noisy exceptions are more common in wet/transition laps.
implementation_hint: Train a dry-only XGBoost specialist with monotone increasing constraints on features like `TyreLife`, `Service_Age_Distance`, `Cannot_Finish_On_Current_Tyre`, `Finish_Stop_Pressure`, and monotone decreasing constraints on `Finish_Life_Margin`. Blend it with an unconstrained model for wet/intermediate compounds.
expected_effect: Can smooth implausible local inversions and improve out-of-race generalization without inventing new raw features.
risk: True F1 strategy is not globally monotone because traffic, warm-up, and Safety Car effects can reverse the decision boundary.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
